title: Boraginella latisepala
taxonomy: Boraginella latisepala is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. This family is a large group of flowering plants commonly known as borage or forget-me-nots. Boraginella latisepala is further classified within the genus Boraginella, which is a small genus of flowering plants native to tropical Africa. The species is believed to be a relatively rare and lesser-known member of the Boraginaceae family. Further research is needed to confirm its classification within the broader botanical community.
uses: Boraginella latisepala is reported to have various medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help in treating wounds and skin infections. Additionally, it is used to treat digestive issues and fever.
